Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) face Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) in Match 23 of IPL 2026 at the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium today, April 15. This encounter features a stark contrast between the tournament’s best batting unit and its most economical bowling attack . RCB enters the match in third place after a high-scoring win over Mumbai Indians, having crossed the 200-run mark in every match this season . LSG, currently seventh, is looking to bounce back after a loss to Gujarat Titans, relying on a bowling unit that has yet to concede 200 runs this year.Lucknow Super Giants have had an inconsistent season, winning as many matches as they’ve lost, largely due to underwhelming performances from key overseas players Mitchell Marsh and Nicholas Pooran. However, unexpected contributors such as Mukul Choudhary and a strong bowling unit led by Mohammed Shami have provided positives.Royal Challengers Bengaluru, meanwhile, have started strongly, winning three of their four matches and looking like a well-settled unit under captain Rajat Patidar, who has been in excellent form, especially against pace. While RCB’s batting has been consistent, their bowling, particularly at the death, remains a concern, with one of the poorest economy rates this season.The M. Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru is renowned as a true batting haven, characterized by its flat deck and notoriously short boundaries that make it one of the highest-scoring venues in the IPL. For today's clash between RCB and LSG, fans can expect the ball to come onto the bat nicely, allowing power-hitters to clear the ropes with ease, as evidenced by the 200+ scores already seen here this season.Phil Salt, Virat Kohli , Devdutt Padikkal, Rajat Patidar, Tim David, Jitesh Sharma, Romario Shepherd, Krunal Pandya, Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Rasikh Salam, Josh HazlewoodImpact Sub: Suyash SharmaAiden Markram, Mitchell Marsh, Rishabh Pant, Ayush Badoni, Nicholas Pooran, Abdul Samad, Mukul Choudhary, George Linde/Anrich Nortje, Mohammed Shami, Avesh Khan, Digvesh Singh RathiImpact Sub: Prince YadavThe weather in Bengaluru for today's IPL match between Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) and Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) is expected to be ideal for cricket, with clear skies and no threat of rain. Since it is an evening game at the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium, conditions will remain dry and pleasant for both players and fans. The temperature is forecast to hover between 21°C and 25°C throughout the match duration.The RCB vs LSG clash will be held at the M.Chinnaswamy Stadium, Bengaluru, with the match set to begin at 7:30 PM IST. The game will be broadcast on the Star Sports Network, while live streaming will be available on JioHotstar.Philip Salt, Virat Kohli, Devdutt Padikkal, Rajat Patidar(c), Jitesh Sharma(w), Tim David, Romario Shepherd, Krunal Pandya, Bhuvneshwar Kumar, Jacob Duffy, Suyash Sharma, Rasikh Salam Dar, Venkatesh Iyer, Jacob Bethell, Swapnil Singh, Mangesh Yadav, Josh Hazlewood, Jordan Cox, Nuwan Thushara, Vicky Ostwal, Vihaan Malhotra, Abhinandan Singh, Kanishk Chouhan, Satvik DeswalAiden Markram, Mitchell Marsh, Rishabh Pant(w/c), Ayush Badoni, Nicholas Pooran, Abdul Samad, Mukul Choudhary, George Linde, Mohammed Shami, Avesh Khan, Digvesh Singh Rathi, Prince Yadav, Manimaran Siddharth, Shahbaz Ahmed, Mayank Yadav, Matthew Breetzke, Anrich Nortje, Josh Inglis, Himmat Singh, Mohsin Khan, Arjun Tendulkar, Akash Maharaj Singh, Akshat Raghuwanshi, Arshin Kulkarni, Naman Tiwari
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) face Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) in Match 23 of IPL 2026 at the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium, Bengaluru. RCB enters the match in third place after a high-scoring win over Mumbai Indians, having crossed the 200-run mark in every match this season. LSG, currently seventh, is looking to bounce back after a loss to Gujarat Titans, relying on a bowling unit that has yet to concede 200 runs this year.

Article Title: Sikandar Raza: Drama in PSL: Zimbabwe's Sikandar Raza fires back at Pakistan's Imad Wasim over 'money' remark
Article Text:
Imad Wasim, Sikandar Raza

Watch How Pakistan trained after embarrassing defeat to India in Colombo How Pakistan trained after embarrassing defeat to India in Colombo

Zimbabwe cricketer Sikandar Raza , who is currently playing for Lahore Qalandars in the Pakistan Super League (PSL) 2026, has responded to remarks earlier made by former Pakistan all-rounder Imad Wasim.The comments date back to last year, when Imad spoke about Raza’s late arrival for the PSL 10 final at Gaddafi Stadium in Lahore. Raza had taken partial leave from the Qalandars squad to play a one-off Test for Zimbabwe against England in Nottingham.That match ended late on Saturday night (Pakistan Standard Time), which put his availability for the final in doubt. However, Raza rejoined the Qalandars just minutes before the toss and took the field against Quetta Gladiators.When Imad was asked to describe Raza in one word, he instead spoke about the demands of franchise cricket.“Like Shoaib Akhtar said — money can do things for you,” Imad had remarked. “If you’re getting paid, you’ll go. I travel a lot too. Sometimes one match ends, and the next day you’re playing another. I have travelled 24 hours straight and gone directly into a match. So yes, money can make different things happen.”In a recent interview with a local sports platform, Raza addressed those comments and said his decision was not about money.He said he had in fact lost money by missing earlier matches.“Regarding Imad Wasim’s comments about money being my motivation to return and play the PSL final — money was not the motivating factor,” Raza said, as quoted by Pakistan-based news outlet Geo Super. “My priorities are respect, honour, and loyalty. In fact, money was deducted from my contract for the matches I missed, so his opinion doesn’t bother me because it isn’t true.”For context, Shaheen Afridi ’s Lahore Qalandars went on to win the PSL 10 title with a six-wicket victory over Quetta Gladiators, securing their third title.Chasing 202, Lahore got off to a quick start through Mohammad Naeem, who scored 47 off 27 balls, including one four and six sixes, before being dismissed by Faheem Ashraf.Abdullah Shafique added 41 off 28 balls, with four boundaries and a six, before being dismissed by Usman Tariq, leaving Lahore at 115 for 3 in 12.3 overs.As the required rate rose, Thisara Perera and Bhanuka Rajapaksa found scoring difficult for a period.Raza then shifted momentum by hitting consecutive boundaries off Mohammad Amir. Perera also contributed with a half-century off 28 balls.With 13 runs needed in the final over, Raza hit a four and a six to seal the win with one ball remaining.
Article Summary: Sikandar Raza has responded to remarks made by former Pakistan all-rounder Imad Wasim. Imad spoke about Raza’s late arrival for the PSL 10 final at Gaddafi Stadium in Lahore. Raza had taken partial leave from the Qalandars squad to play a one-off Test for Zimbabwe against England in Nottingham.

Article Title: Atletico Madrid: Champions League: Atletico Madrid edge Barcelona 3-2 on aggregate to enter semis
Article Text:
Julian Alvarez of Atletico de Madrid takes a free kick during the UEFA Champions League 2025/26 Quarter-Final Second Leg match between Club Atlético de Madrid and FC Barcelona at Riyadh Air Metropolitano on April 14, 2026 in Madrid, Spain. (Photo/Getty Images)

Atletico Madrid knocked Barcelona out of the Champions League despite a 2-1 defeat in the second leg of their quarter-final on Tuesday, advancing 3-2 on aggregate.Barcelona took an early lead through Lamine Yamal and Ferran Torres inside the first 24 minutes. However, Ademola Lookman scored for Atletico, which proved enough after their 2-0 win in the first leg.Managed by Diego Simeone, Atletico reached the semi-finals of the competition for the first time since 2017.Barcelona’s hopes of forcing extra time were affected late in the match when defender Eric Garcia was sent off for bringing down Alexander Sorloth as he ran towards goal.Atletico, who have not won the competition and lost the finals in 2014 and 2016 under Simeone, will now face either Arsenal or Sporting Lisbon in the semi-finals.“(We're) very happy, knocking out a great Barca side. We had a great game away... it was really hard for us at the start (of this one), but the team knew how to get back on its feet,” Atletico midfielder Koke told Movistar.Barcelona last won the Champions League in 2015, and their wait for another title continues. Midfielder Frenkie de Jong said the team is improving despite the exit.“I think we had a very good game, we gave our lives out there, we tried everything,” he said.“I feel like luck was not on our side. We have to continue -- we're on a good path, we're growing every year.”Barcelona coach Hansi Flick started with Torres and Gavi in attack, leaving Marcus Rashford and Robert Lewandowski on the bench.Barcelona began strongly, with Yamal scoring in the fourth minute after pressing Clement Lenglet into an error. Torres later doubled the lead in the 24th minute after finishing a pass from Dani Olmo.Atletico responded in the 31st minute when Marcos Llorente set up Lookman to score.Barcelona thought they had a third goal in the second half through Torres, but it was ruled out for offside.Goalkeeper Joan Garcia made a save to deny Robin Le Normand, while tensions increased as Matteo Ruggieri was left bleeding after contact with Gavi.The match turned further against Barcelona when Eric Garcia was sent off late in the game. Flick responded by pushing Ronald Araujo forward, but Atletico held on through stoppage time to secure their place in the semi-finals.
Article Summary: Atletico Madrid beat Barcelona 2-1 in Champions League quarter-final. Lamine Yamal and Ferran Torres scored for Barcelona. Ademola Lookman scored for Atletico. Atletico advance 3-2 on aggregate and will face either Arsenal or Sporting Lisbon.

Article Title: Champions League: Barcelona eye Atletico comeback
Article Text:
Barcelona's Marcus Rashford, left, celebrates after scoring his side's fourth goal with Lamine Yamal, right, and Pedri during the Spanish La Liga soccer match between Barcelona and Espanyol in Barcelona, Spain, Saturday, April 11, 2026. (AP Photo)

Watch Football returns to Delhi! SC Delhi gear up for first ISL home game Football returns to Delhi! SC Delhi gear up for first ISL home game

1ST LEG SCORES

Barcelona: Marcus Rashford had his chance against Atletico Madrid, but failed to take it — or any of the next five that came along on a frustrating European night.On Tuesday the England international has another opportunity to try and inspire Barcelona to a Champions League comeback in the Spanish capital.Rashford fired blanks as Barca suffered a 2-0 quarterfinal first leg defeat, but the England international was also his team’s brightest spark against the Rojiblancos, even though the killer finish eluded him. With Raphinha out injured, Rashford has been the natural choice on Barca’s left flank.Talented teenager Lamine Yamal was double and triple marked, in the first leg, to keep him under wraps. Atletico Madrid midfielder Koke said his team must play with “personality” to stop Barcelona mounting a comeback.“It’s hugely important to beat the press they usually put on,” Koke said on Monday. “They’re a team that presses very high, that pushes their line almost up to the halfway line that suffocates you.“That’s where our personality comes in, when it’s time to play, to want the ball, to make ourselves available, to make runs in behind.”Barcelona 0 vs Atletico Madrid 2 PSG 2 vs Liverpool 0
Article Summary: Rashford fired blanks as Barca suffered a 2-0 quarterfinal first leg defeat. England international was also his team’s brightest spark against the Rojiblancos. With Raphinha out injured, Rashford has been the natural choice on Barca's left flank.Talented teenager Lamine Yamal was double and triple marked, in the first leg, to keep him under wraps.

Article Title: Footballer dies after robbers attack team bus
Article Text:
Football (Representational Photo)

Watch EXCLUSIVE: Where did things go wrong for India in Women's Asia Cup? Aditi Chauhan decodes EXCLUSIVE: Where did things go wrong for India in Women's Asia Cup? Aditi Chauhan decodes

Poll Do you believe community support could help prevent such tragic incidents? Yes, community vigilance is crucial Not necessarily, crime happens everywhere

NEW DELHI: A Ghanaian Premier League footballer, Dominic Frimpong, has died after armed robbers attacked his team while they were returning from an away match on Sunday, according to the Ghana Football Association.The incident shocked the country’s football community and left his club and teammates devastated.The club, Berekum Chelsea FC, said in a statement that "masked men wielding guns and assault rifles" fired on the team bus and forced the players to take cover in the bushes.The attack reportedly happened around 10:30 pm local time along the Ahyiresu-Kwame Dwumor Sreso road in the Nyinahin District of Ghana’s Ashanti Region.During the chaos, Dominic Frimpong was hit by gunfire. Police said he was rushed to hospital but later died while receiving treatment.The violent robbery also affected others, including a 52-year-old victim. "Another victim, George Owusu Afriyie, 52, was also robbed of 4,500.00 cedis ($408)," the police said in a statement.Authorities confirmed the incident and described it as a criminal attack that led to loss of life and theft.The Ghana Football Association called the event "tragic" and a "huge loss to Berekum Chelsea but also to Ghana football as a whole."Frimpong, just 20 years old, was seen as a rising talent in Ghanaian football."Dominic was a promising young talent whose dedication and passion for the game embodied the spirit of our league," it said in a statement.He had joined Berekum Chelsea FC on loan from Aduana Stars FC earlier this year and had already played 13 matches, scoring two goals.His sudden death has left the local football community mourning a young career cut short in a violent and unexpected tragedy.
Article Summary: Ghanaian Premier League footballer, Dominic Frimpong, has died after armed robbers attacked his team. The club, Berekum Chelsea FC, said in a statement that "masked men wielding guns and assault rifles" fired on the team bus and forced the players to take cover in the bushes. Police said he was rushed to hospital but later died while receiving treatment.

Article Title: Manchester City rout Chelsea to close gap on leaders Arsenal
Article Text:
PTI Photo

- Cherki sparkles -

Manchester City ignited the Premier League title race with a second-half blitz that sealed a 3-0 win against Chelsea as they closed the gap on leaders Arsenal on Sunday. Pep Guardiola's side produced a devastating spell immediately after half-time, with Nico O'Reilly and Marc Guehi scoring in the space of six minutes at Stamford Bridge.Jeremy Doku wrapped up a statement victory to ensure City took advantage of Arsenal's shock 2-1 home defeat against Bournemouth on Saturday.Second-placed City are now only six points behind Arsenal with a game in hand, setting up a seismic showdown against the Gunners at the Etihad Stadium on April 19.Having beaten Arsenal in the League Cup final and thrashed Liverpool in the FA Cup quarter-finals, City are hitting form at just the right time.Their bid for a seventh English title under Guardiola appeared to be fading after successive draws with strugglers West Ham and Nottingham Forest in their previous two league matches.A disjointed first half against Chelsea suggested City might waste a golden opportunity to pile pressure on Arsenal.But Guardiola's half-time team-talk had the desired effect and City can now target next weekend's do-or-die visit from Mikel Arteta's spluttering team.If City win that blockbuster clash, they will be within three points of Arsenal, who blew substantial leads in the 2023 and 2024 title races, allowing Guardiola's men to pip them to the trophy.Arteta said the defeat to Bournemouth was a "punch in the face" and City's success in west London was another body blow for the Spaniard.City have won 29 of their 32 league games in the month of April in recent seasons, underlining their ability to thrive when the title pressure mounts.After winning their first four league matches following Liam Rosenior's arrival from Strasbourg to replace Enzo Maresca, Chelsea have won just one of their last seven, losing three in a row to leave the Blues boss facing some difficult questions.Chelsea, who haven't beaten City since the 2021 Champions League final, were again without Enzo Fernandez after Rosenior's controversial decision to drop the Argentine midfielder for hinting he might leave in the summer.Sixth-placed Chelsea looked subdued without Fernandez and they trail four points behind Liverpool in the race to qualify for the Champions League via a top-five finish.Lacking energy and cohesion in a sloppy start, City were fortunate not to fall behind when Marc Cucurella's clinical finish was disallowed for a tight offside.City also let Pedro Neto in far too easily for a stinging strike that forced Gianluigi Donnarumma to save at his near post.It took City 35 minutes to mount an incisive raid as Bernardo Silva stretched to meet O'Reilly's cross, but Robert Sanchez made a fine save from the midfielder's close-range effort.But City stepped up after the interval and O'Reilly made the breakthrough in the 51st minute.Rayan Cherki whipped an in-swinging cross towards O'Reilly and the City left-back reprised his League Cup final heroics with another clinical header from close range.Six minutes later, Cherki again showed the creative genius that has won over Guardiola despite some impetuous moments in his first season in Manchester.The France playmaker glided past a gaggle of Chelsea players on the edge of the area before threading a sublime pass to Guehi, who looked more like a forward than a centre-back as he smashed a perfect strike into the far corner from 12 yards.City benefitted from wretched Chelsea defending for their third goal in the 68th minute.Sanchez rolled the ball to Moises Caicedo even though the Chelsea midfielder was surrounded by three City players and Doku pounced, racing into the area to drill home as Guardiola celebrated a priceless result.
Article Summary: Manchester City beat Chelsea 3-0 at Stamford Bridge. Nico O'Reilly, Marc Guehi and Jeremy Doku on target. Second-placed City now six points behind Arsenal with a game in hand. Pep Guardiola's side face Gunners at the Etihad Stadium on April 19.

Article Title: Bournemouth stun Arsenal 2-1 at the Emirates to dent Premier League title push
Article Text:
Bournemouth's Evanilson, and Eli Junior Kroupi celebrate (AP Photo/Dave Shopland)

Arsenal’s Premier League title hopes suffered a major setback on Saturday as they were beaten 2-1 at the Emirates by Bournemouth, handing Manchester City renewed belief in the race at the top of the table.Bournemouth struck first in the 17th minute when Junior Kroupi pounced on a loose ball inside the box after a looping deflection off William Saliba fell kindly into his path, allowing him to tap home from close range and silence the home crowd.Arsenal responded and gradually grew into the game, with their pressure finally telling in the 35th minute. Viktor Gyökeres stepped up and smashed home a penalty after Ryan Christie was adjudged to have handled the ball inside the area. That goal brought Arsenal level and briefly settled nerves around the stadium.The Gunners came out stronger after the break, and Mikel Arteta reacted with bold attacking changes, introducing Eberechi Eze, 16-year-old Max Dowman and Leandro Trossard in place of Kai Havertz, Noni Madueke and Gabriel Martinelli. However, despite the fresh legs, Arsenal continued to struggle to break down Bournemouth’s disciplined defensive structure.Bournemouth, however, remained dangerous in transition and regained the lead in the 74th minute with a well-worked move. David Brooks created the opening before Evanilson’s involvement helped the ball fall to Alex Scott , who finished emphatically, drilling a powerful shot past David Raya.Arsenal threw everything forward in the closing stages but lacked precision in the final third. Gyökeres came closest late on, firing wide deep into stoppage time, as Bournemouth held firm under pressure.The hosts ended with just three shots on target and suffered only their second home league defeat of the season, a worrying sign given the timing of the run-in.Arsenal remain nine points clear at the top with six games left in their pursuit of a first Premier League title since 2004, but Manchester City, with games in hand, now have a clear opportunity to cut the gap and pile pressure on the leaders ahead of their upcoming meeting at the Etihad.
Article Summary: Bournemouth struck first in the 17th minute when Junior Kroupi pounced on a loose ball inside the box. Arsenal responded and gradually grew into the game, with their pressure finally telling in the 35th minute. Viktor Gyökeres stepped up and smashed home a penalty after Ryan Christie was adjudged to have handled the ball.

Article Title: Ollie Watkins helps Aston Villa defeat Bologna in quarter-final of Europa League
Article Text:
Aston Villa beat Bologna (Screengrab)

Aston Villa moved a step closer to the Europa League semi-finals with a hard-fought 3-1 victory over Bologna on Thursday night, in a contest that could have taken a very different turn had it not been for the clinical finishing of Ollie Watkins.Villa’s win was built on key moments at either end of the match. Ezri Konsa gave the visitors the lead just before half-time, while Watkins struck twice, once early in the second half and again deep into stoppage time, to put Unai Emery’s side firmly in control of the tie. The result leaves Villa strong favourites to progress to the last four, where they will face either FC Porto or Nottingham Forest, provided they complete the job in the second leg at Villa Park. Despite the defeat, Bologna remain in contention thanks to a late goal from Jonathan Rowe.Bologna entered the match in excellent form, having won their last seven away games in all competitions, and they began with intent. The hosts came agonisingly close to taking the lead on multiple occasions, striking the woodwork twice during a dominant first-half spell. They even thought they had gone ahead in the 26th minute when a deflection involving Konsa resulted in the ball finding the net.However, celebrations were cut short after VAR ruled that Santiago Castro had been marginally offside in the build-up.Moments later, Bologna continued to threaten, with Rowe’s energetic run down the left setting up Lewis Ferguson, whose effort crashed against the underside of the crossbar.But just as Bologna looked the more dangerous side, Villa struck a decisive blow. On the stroke of half-time, Konsa rose strongly in the box, holding off Jhon Lucumí before heading home from a corner delivered by Youri Tielemans. The goal, aided by a misjudgement from goalkeeper Federico Ravaglia, shifted the momentum firmly in Villa’s favour.Villa capitalised on that momentum early in the second half. Just five minutes after the restart, Watkins doubled the lead after Bologna defender Torbjorn Heggem carelessly gave away possession inside his own penalty area. The England striker remained composed, slotting the ball through Ravaglia’s legs to make it 2-0.Despite the setback, Bologna refused to back down. Federico Bernardeschi came close to pulling one back, striking the outside of the post as the hosts continued to press forward. Their persistence was finally rewarded in the 90th minute when Rowe produced a fine finish to give Bologna a lifeline and spark hopes of a comeback in the second leg.However, any momentum the hosts gained was quickly extinguished. Deep into stoppage time, Villa struck again from a set piece, with Watkins left completely unmarked to tap home his second goal of the night and restore the two-goal cushion.Speaking after the match, Watkins highlighted the importance of timing in Villa’s goals. “Just before half-time is a great time to score. We got the second after half-time and then felt we could always score more,” he said. “At this stage of the season, I’m raring to go. I feel like I could play another 90 minutes, I’m hungry for what’s next.”Villa’s clinical edge ultimately proved to be the difference. While Bologna created chances and showed attacking intent, their inability to convert, and costly defensive errors, allowed Emery’s experienced side to take control of the tie. With the second leg set to be played in Birmingham, Villa now have one foot in the semi-finals and are firmly on course to chase their first major trophy in three decades, as well as strengthen their push for a top-five finish in the Premier League
Article Summary: Aston Villa beat Bologna 3-1 in their Europa League quarter-final first leg. Ezri Konsa, Ollie Watkins and Jonathan Rowe scored for the visitors. Villa will now face either FC Porto or Nottingham Forest in the last four.

Article Title: Atletico Madrid beat 10-man Barcelona 2-0 to take control of quarter-final
Article Text:
Atletico Madrid's Julian Alvarez, right, takes a free kick during the Champions League quarterfinal first leg soccer match between Barcelona and Atletico Madrid in Barcelona, Spain, Wednesday, April 8, 2026. (AP Photo/Joan Monfort)

Atletico Madrid moved a step closer to the Champions League semi-finals with a commanding 2-0 victory over Barcelona in the first leg of their quarter-final clash at Camp Nou. Goals from Julian Alvarez and Alexander Sorloth, combined with a pivotal red card for Barcelona, gave Diego Simeone’s side a significant advantage heading into the return leg.Barcelona, currently leading La Liga, began the match on the front foot and looked the more composed side in the early stages of this all-Spanish encounter. However, the game took a dramatic turn when Pau Cubarsi was dismissed after bringing down Giuliano Simeone, who was through on goal. Although the referee initially showed a yellow card, VAR intervention upgraded it to a red, leaving the hosts with 10 men before half-time.Atletico capitalised immediately. Alvarez stepped up to take the resulting free-kick and delivered a stunning strike into the top corner, putting the visitors ahead. The goal shifted the momentum firmly in Atletico’s favour, even though Barcelona continued to show resilience despite being a man down.In the second half, Barcelona pushed hard to find an equaliser. Marcus Rashford, who has been in lively form, posed a constant threat down the left flank and created multiple opportunities.However, Atletico’s defence held firm, and goalkeeper Juan Musso made crucial interventions when needed.Against the run of play, Atletico struck again. A well-worked attacking move involving Antoine Griezmann and Matteo Ruggieri ended with a precise cross into the box, where Sorloth used his physical presence to outmuscle his marker and finish confidently past the goalkeeper, doubling Atletico’s lead with around 20 minutes remaining.Barcelona’s players and coaching staff were left frustrated not only by the result but also by certain officiating decisions. Head coach Hansi Flick expressed dissatisfaction with VAR, particularly questioning why Cubarsi’s challenge was reviewed while a possible handball incident involving Atletico defender Marc Pubill was not examined.Despite their numerical disadvantage, Barcelona continued to fight and created several chances, with Rashford coming close on multiple occasions. However, they were unable to find the breakthrough and ultimately suffered their first home defeat since Camp Nou reopened in November 2025.For Atletico Madrid, the result carries added significance. It marks Diego Simeone’s first-ever win at Camp Nou in his 14-year tenure and the club’s first victory at the stadium since 2006. Having already eliminated Barcelona from the Copa del Rey semi-finals earlier this year, Atletico now hold a strong psychological and tactical edge.Speaking after the match, Antoine Griezmann urged caution despite the positive result, emphasising that the tie is far from over and that the team must remain focused ahead of the second leg.Barcelona, who last lifted the Champions League trophy in 2015, now face a daunting task in the return leg in Madrid. They will need a remarkable comeback to overturn the deficit and keep their European ambitions alive.Meanwhile, Atletico Madrid, two-time finalists in 2014 and 2016, are now in a strong position as they continue their pursuit of a first-ever Champions League title.
Article Summary: Atletico Madrid beat Barcelona 2-0 in Champions League quarter-final first leg. Goals from Julian Alvarez and Alexander Sorloth gave Diego Simeone's side a significant advantage heading into the return leg. Barcelona's Pau Cubarsi was sent off after bringing down Giuliano Sime one.

Article Title: AIFF’s ‘non-negotiable’ floodlight rule puts Indian football club in crisis
Article Text:
Representational photo of a floodlight (Photo by Steve Bardens/Getty Images)

Watch ISL commercial rights: AIFF get two different bids ISL commercial rights: AIFF get two different bids

RUFC to meet govt officials

Poll Should Rajasthan United FC switch their home venue due to the floodlight requirement? Yes, find a new venue No, stay at Vidyadhar Nagar Stadium

NEW DELHI: In the middle of an already delayed Indian Football League (IFL) season, Rajasthan United will meet the government officials to request an alternate home venue. The club, second in the IFL standings, have been put in a tough spot following All India Football Federation's (AIFF) directive of organising matches under floodlights from April 19 onwards.Rajasthan United play their home games at Jaipur's Vidyadhar Nagar Stadium, which was built by Rajasthan State Sports Council and Jaipur Development Authority. The venue doesn't have floodlights installed.In a letter to the IFL clubs, AIFF asked the club's "cooperation" to address the "league's broadcast commitments and commercial standing."The letter made clear that starting April 19, all IFL matches will kick off at 6:30 pm IST."Since all forthcoming fixtures will be held post-sunset, fully operational floodlights at the match venue will be an absolute and non-negotiable requirement for every fixture going forward," stated the letter.The AIFF further asked that clubs find alternative venues in case their current home grounds are unable to host evening fixtures.In case a club is unable to host a game with fully operational floodlights, they will be fined Rs 9 lakh for every game missed."...we must be transparent if any club that fails to comply with the 6:30 PM kick-off requirement, or plays a match at a venue without fully operational floodlights, will be liable to a financial penalty of INR 9,00,000/- (Rupees Nine Lakhs Only) per instance," said the letter.The directive comes more than halfway into the IFL season, with all teams having played seven matches each.A club official told TimesofIndia.com that they're going to speak to the government and seek a solution. It is not the first time that the club have approached the state government on the matter.If they fail in this endeavour, their other options include discussions with AIFF to play their home games at 5 pm instead. The other option includes playing their home games at Namdhari FC or Dempo SC’s grounds.Rajasthan United FC have won four of their five home games in the ongoing IFL season, picking 13 points from a possible 15.They are one of three clubs to have not lost a home game so far, the others being Sreenidi Deccan FC and Dempo FC, but they've earned fewer points.Rajasthan United currently occupy second place in the IFL points table with 14 points from seven matches played, trailing Diamond Harbour Football Club by two points.
Article Summary: Rajasthan United will meet the government officials to request an alternate home venue. All India Football Federation (AIFF) directive of organising matches under floodlights from April 19 onwards. AIFF asked clubs to find alternative venues in case their current home grounds are unable to host evening fixtures.

Article Title: India U12 football team crowned champions at Real Madrid Foundation World Challenge
Article Text:
India U12 Football Team

Watch ISL commercial rights: AIFF get two different bids ISL commercial rights: AIFF get two different bids

Global Stage, Elite Competition

Pan-India Representation and Key Wins

India’s Under-12 football team scripted a historic triumph by emerging champions at the prestigious Real Madrid Foundation World Challenge, held at Real Madrid’s iconic training complex in Madrid, Spain. Competing against top youth teams from across the globe, the Indian side delivered a dominant and composed campaign, culminating in a commanding 3-0 victory over Brazil in the final. Showcasing a blend of tactical discipline and attacking flair, the young Indian team stood out against some of the strongest footballing nations.The victory holds significant importance given the scale and stature of the tournament, which is hosted by one of Europe’s biggest football clubs. It reflects the steady growth of India’s grassroots football structure and sets a new benchmark for youth development in the country.Held annually during Easter week, the tournament featured over 80 teams and more than 1,000 young players across U10, U12, and U14 categories. Teams from Europe, North America, South America, and South Asia participated, making it a truly global event.The matches were played at Real Madrid’s famed Sports City, a venue associated with global stars like Kylian Mbappé and Vinícius Júnior, offering young players a rare chance to compete on world-class pitches.India’s success was not limited to the U12 category. The U14 team also impressed by reaching the semi-finals, where they narrowly lost to Italy in a closely contested match decided by a late goal.Both squads displayed resilience, skill, and depth, underlining India’s growing competitiveness at the international youth level.The Indian teams featured players from across the country, including Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Pune, Bengaluru, and Hyderabad, representing a unified effort under the Conscient Sports program. Their journey included notable wins against teams from Spain, Germany, Brazil, Uruguay, Mexico, the USA, Canada, Malaysia, Azerbaijan, and Morocco, highlighting the high level of competition.Adding further prestige to the event, Brazil and Real Madrid legend Roberto Carlos attended as the official ambassador, interacting with players and inspiring the next generation of footballers.
Article Summary: India’s Under-12 football team scripted a historic triumph by emerging champions at the prestigious Real Madrid Foundation World Challenge. Competing against top youth teams from across the globe, the Indian side delivered a dominant and composed campaign, culminating in a commanding 3-0 victory over Brazil in the final. The victory holds significant importance given the scale and stature of the tournament.

Article Title: Crispin Chettri set to return as women’s national football team coach
Article Text:
Crispin Chettri

Watch Football returns to Delhi! SC Delhi gear up for first ISL home game Football returns to Delhi! SC Delhi gear up for first ISL home game

PANAJI: A little over two months after being asked to step aside to make way for a foreign coach at the AFC Asian Cup 2026, Crispin Chettri is set to return as head coach of the women’s national team. In its first meeting after the Asian Cup, the All India Football Federation’s technical committee led by former India captain IM Vijayan suggested that Amelia Valverde’s contract should not be extended. The Costa Rican was brought in specifically to guide India at the continental showpiece in Australia where eight FIFA World Cup spots were on offer.India exited without a point, and the technical committee, in its latest meeting, felt it was best to revert to a domestic coach for the next two assignments: FIFA Series in Kenya and the SAFF Women’s Championship.“Crispin was a natural choice since he has already been in-charge of the national team,” a technical committee member told TOI on Saturday.“There were options, but we could not look beyond Crispin since there’s little time for any experiment.”
Article Summary: Crispin Chettri is set to return as head coach of the women's national team. Amelia Valverde’s contract should not be extended, says All India Football Federation. The technical committee felt it was best to revert to a domestic coach for the next two assignments.

Article Title: Melanie Collins, NFL sideline reporter stuns fans with swimsuit photo, building hype even before 2026 season starts
Article Text:
melanie collins (Instagram)

Why is Melanie Collins trending ahead of the NFL Draft



What does Melanie Collins' career reveal about the reality of sideline reporting



Melanie Collins isn’t waiting for kickoff in the 2026 NFL season to get people talking. The CBS Sports sideline reporter drew attention this week after sharing vacation photos from St. Lucia, a moment that quickly rippled through social media. While the 2026 NFL Draft looms, Collins has stepped away briefly from the grind, offering a glimpse of the life behind the camera. The reaction was immediate, but it also highlighted something deeper about her growing visibility in a demanding role.The timing says a lot. With the draft just days away, most NFL coverage is locked into speculation and roster math. Collins managed to break through that noise without saying a word about football. Her photos prompted a wave of reactions, including a simple “Unreal” from Ashley Nicole Moss, while fans filled the comments with praise like “Absolutely stunning” and “Howwwww are you real.”It wasn’t manufactured attention. Collins has built a steady presence since joining CBS in 2018, working high-profile broadcasts with a calm, prepared style that doesn’t chase headlines.Moments like this feel organic, a byproduct of familiarity rather than a shift in persona. Viewers know her from the sidelines. Social media just adds another layer, one that shows a more relaxed version of the same person.Melanie Collins’ path to this point wasn’t accidental. Before CBS, she covered global events for Yahoo Sports, including the Olympics, World Cup, Final Four, and NBA Finals. She even had a brief run on ESPN’s “First Take.” The résumé is broad, but the job itself comes with a cost that often goes unnoticed.“It’s disturbing how many hours of my life I’ve spent sitting on planes,” Collins said.That line lands because it strips away the gloss. Sideline reporting looks glamorous on television, but it runs on constant travel, long weeks, and missed holidays. Collins moves from city to city through the heart of the NFL calendar, adjusting to different crews, teams, and storylines with little downtime.Still, she doesn’t frame it as a burden. She’s clear about the trade-off, calling the experience “worth it” despite the demands that stretch from August to February. That balance between visibility and sacrifice defines her role more than any viral moment.
Article Summary: CBS Sports sideline reporter Melanie Collins shared vacation photos from St. Lucia. The photos prompted a wave of reactions, including a simple “Unreal” from Ashley Nicole Moss. Collins has built a steady presence since joining CBS in 2018, working high-profile broadcasts.

Article Title: Travis Kelce And Taylor Swift Wedding: “Tay loves a sense of humour”: Internet can’t get enough of Travis Kelce’s goofy moment as July wedding buzz with Taylor Swift takes center stage
Article Text:
Internet can’t get enough of Travis Kelce’s goofy moment as Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding takes center stage (Getty Images)

Travis Kelce goofy clip goes viral again as fans connect it to Taylor Swift



Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's July wedding plans bring guest list, surprises and strict rules into attention

A hilarious clip has suddenly put Travis Kelce back in the spotlight. The Kansas City Chiefs star is trending, not for football, but for a hilarious old video that fans cannot stop sharing. The current situation has only made it bigger. It comes just as Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding talks start heating up ahead of July.The internet moved fast. Within hours, the clip spread all over social media. Fans began linking the moment to Kelce’s personality and his relationship with Swift. One viral line now sums up the mood, and it ties directly to why this video is getting so much attention.The video comes from Catching Kelce, a reality show Travis Kelce did early in his career. In the clip, the star TE is part of a group workout session. He wears a bright neon tank top, short green shorts, a headband, and sneakers. In that gym, Kelce joined several women dancing around him.Kelce does not hold back. He follows the routine but adds his own style. Some moves look off beat, but he keeps going with full confidence. That is what makes the clip funny. It feels real, not staged.The video picked up traction again. A fan account captioned it, “he is marrying Taylor Swift.”That sarcastic line spread fast and drew multiple reactions.A fan said, “It’s from Catching Kelce 😭,” adding context.Soon after, a fan comment hinted at pop icons' rare liking, and it stood out: “And this is one of the reasons why Tay loves a sense of humour.”At the same time, not everyone liked it. One user wrote, “Not cool. That man would like that to stay in the past.” Still, the reactions kept the video trending.Despite the nonstop reactions, the clip has gone viral because Kelce and pop icon Taylor Swift are expected to get married in July, which is only two months away.Now, the bigger story continues to grow. The Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding is expected to take place on July 3 in New York City, according to Page Six on April 13, 2026. It will be a multi-day event, not just a single ceremony. Guests have already received invitations to keep the entire weekend free.The guest list will include some major names. Close friends like Selena Gomez and Gigi Hadid are expected to play key roles. Reports say they are planning special moments, including a performance and a short video about Swift’s journey and her relationship with Kelce.There is more. A bachelorette celebration is also in the works, with fun segments planned for the couple. The Sun reported on April 11, 2026, that Kelce has arranged a series of elaborate flower deliveries for Swift in the lead-up to the wedding. Each one is expected to be more special than the last.Privacy will be strict. Reports suggest mobile phones could be banned during the ceremony. Security will also be high due to the scale of the event.All of this keeps Travis Kelce in focus. The viral clip adds another layer to the story. It shows his personality simply.As the Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding gets closer, even an old video has found new life. And right now, that moment explains why fans believe humor is a big part of their relationship.
Article Summary: The video comes from Catching Kelce, a reality show Travis Kelce did early in his career. In the clip, the star TE is part of a group workout session. He wears a bright neon tank top, short green shorts, a headband, and sneakers. Fans began linking the moment to Kelce’s personality and his relationship with Swift.

Article Title: Sherrone Moore punishment: Ex-Michigan coach sentenced to 18 months' probation, no jailtime
Article Text:
Sherrone Moore avoids jail time (Imagn Images)

Why did Sherrone Moore avoid jail time despite the seriousness of the case



What does this sentencing mean for Sherrone Moore’s career and Michigan’s program



The fall of Sherrone Moore reached a legal endpoint Tuesday, but the story around him remains unsettled. Once trusted with one of college football’s most visible programs, Moore avoided jail time after pleading no contest to misdemeanor charges tied to a volatile incident involving a former staffer. A judge in Ann Arbor handed down 18 months’ probation, fines, and strict behavioral conditions. The ruling closes the criminal case, yet it leaves behind difficult questions about accountability, institutional oversight, and how quickly a career can unravel.Judge Cedric Simpson made it clear the outcome was not an endorsement of Moore’s actions but a measured decision based on the full record. “I don't believe, when I look at the entirety of this situation, that incarceration should be appropriate,” Simpson said, while warning, “should there be a violation, all bets are off.”The court noted Moore’s plea to lesser charges after prosecutors dropped more serious counts that did not fully align with the evidence. Simpson also pointed to the mutual communication history between Moore and Paige Shiver, including access she had given him to her apartment. That context, while not excusing the incident, shaped how the case was ultimately prosecuted.Still, the judge did not soften his stance on Moore’s conduct.“Frankly, Mr. Moore, you had no right to do what you did,” Simpson said. “I know she was placed in fear. It was a traumatic experience for you -- it was certainly a traumatic experience for her -- but you had no right to spread your pain to her.”Shiver, who did not attend the hearing, issued a statement afterward that cut through the legal framing: “He broke into my apartment, crying, yelling, enraged, and came at me with knives. I was threatened, and I feared for my life.” She added the sentence “does not reflect the harm done to me.”For Moore, the legal terms are only part of the consequence. His dismissal from the University of Michigan in December had already severed his path in major college football, at least for now. Once the offensive coordinator under Jim Harbaugh during a national title run, Moore briefly held the top job and posted a 16-8 record. That trajectory has now stalled, replaced by mandated counseling, restricted behavior, and a public record that will follow him.In court, Sherrone Moore struck a restrained tone, thanking his support system and saying, “I've taken this process very seriously.” His attorney emphasized therapy and a renewed focus on family, framing the incident as a breaking point rather than a defining trait.Yet the hearing repeatedly circled back to the role of his wife, Kelli Moore, whose actions on the day of the incident drew unusual attention from the bench. Simpson underscored that point with a pointed observation: “The person who is saving you from the full wrath of this court is the person who you betrayed.” He later added, “I am amazed by you, Ms. Moore… If we had more people like you in the world, I may be out of a job, but the world would be a better place. ”For Michigan, the case lands amid broader scrutiny of its athletic department culture. The university has already commissioned external reviews following multiple controversies, signaling concern that this incident is not isolated.Moore’s story now sits in that larger context. The wins and losses are still on record, but they are no longer the headline.
Article Summary: Sherryrone Moore avoided jail time after pleading no contest to misdemeanor charges. A judge in Ann Arbor handed down 18 months’ probation, fines, and strict behavioral conditions. The ruling closes the criminal case, yet it leaves behind difficult questions.

Article Title: Dianna Russini controversy deepens as The Athletic editor admits investigation uncovered more questions than clarity
Article Text:
Dianna Russini quits The Athletic (Getty Images)

Why did Dianna Russini resign from The Athletic amid the Mike Vrabel controversy?



What questions did The Athletic’s investigation raise about journalistic ethics?



Dianna Russini’s sudden exit from The Athletic has left the NFL media circuit grappling with more than just optics. What began as a tabloid-style photo leak involving Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel quickly evolved into a newsroom issue about ethics, perception, and accountability. The images, published by New York Post, triggered an internal review that ended with Russini stepping away before the process concluded. The timing, just ahead of the NFL Draft, has only sharpened the focus on how this unfolded behind the scenes.Dianna Russini’s resignation was not framed as an admission but as a decision to step out of what she described as a spiraling situation. In her letter to executive editor Steven Ginsberg, she acknowledged the outlet’s early backing, writing: “When the Page Six item first appeared, The Athletic supported me unequivocally, expressed confidence in my work and pride in my journalism. For that I am grateful.”That support, however, came under strain as scrutiny intensified. Photos showing Russini and Vrabel together at an Arizona resort raised questions about proximity between a reporter and a high-profile NFL figure she covered. Both maintained the interactions were harmless and taken out of context. Vrabel himself dismissed the narrative, saying, “These photos show a completely innocent interaction and any suggestion otherwise is laughable.Still, the issue was no longer about what the photos showed, but what they suggested. The Athletic’s own editorial standards, aligned with The New York Times, stress that even the appearance of partiality can erode trust. That distinction became central as the story gained traction beyond sports pages and into broader media discourse.Internally, the tone shifted as more details surfaced. Ginsberg told staff that while the initial instinct was to stand by Russini, “new questions were raised” as the review progressed. Those questions were never publicly detailed, but they were significant enough to extend the investigation and bench Russini from NFL coverage in the interim.The scrutiny reflects a deeper tension in modern sports journalism, where access often overlaps with personal familiarity. League insiders and reporters operate in tight circles, especially around marquee events. But newsroom policies remain clear. The Times’ handbook states, “Relationships with sources require sound judgment and self discipline to prevent the fact or appearance of partiality.” It goes further, warning that “romantic involvement with a news source would foster an appearance of partiality. ”Russini’s case now sits at that intersection of policy and perception. While she rejected what she called “self-feeding speculation that is simply unmoored from the facts,” the broader issue had already taken hold. The investigation into her work is set to continue, even after her departure.Meanwhile, Vrabel has kept a low profile, with the New England Patriots maintaining a business-as-usual stance. With the draft approaching, his focus appears unchanged, even as the media storm around him lingers.
Article Summary: Dianna Russini’s sudden exit from The Athletic has left the NFL media circuit grappling with more than just optics. Photos showing Russini and Mike Vrabel together at an Arizona resort raised questions about proximity between a reporter and a high-profile NFL figure she covered. The scrutiny reflects a deeper tension in modern sports journalism.

Article Title: "Self-feeding speculation": Dianna Russini speaks out as she leaves The Athletic amid Mike Vrabel hotel controversy
Article Text:
Dianna Russini resigned from The Athletic following controversy linked to Mike Vrabel. In her resignation letter, she strongly defended her work, stating she stands by her reporting and professionalism. Russini criticized media speculation, calling it inaccurate and detached from facts. Despite the situation, she acknowledged support from her employer. With over 15 years in sports journalism, her departure marks a significant moment in her otherwise respected and accomplished career.

Dianna Russini stands firm as speculation spirals beyond control

The fallout from the growing fight between Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini has taken a sharp and unexpected turn. What started as a widely talked-about sighting in public has now turned into a big career choice. Russini has officially left her job at The Athletic. She chose to leave before things could get worse. The move comes at a bad time, just a few weeks before her contract was set to end.The Associated Press got her resignation, which she sent to executive editor Steven Ginsberg, and it is clear that she is trying to take back control of the story. As rumors spread across different media, Russini chose to deal with the situation head-on instead of staying in the spotlight.In her resignation letter, Russini made it clear that she does not question her work or integrity. “I have covered the NFL with professionalism and dedication throughout my career, and I stand behind every story I have ever published,” wrote Dianna Russini in her resignation letter.Her words reflect both pride and frustration. While acknowledging support from her employer, she added, “When the Page Six item first appeared, The Athletic supported me unequivocally, expressed confidence in my work and pride in my journalism.For that I am grateful.”The controversy traces back to a reported sighting of Russini and Vrabel at a hotel in Arizona. Though details remained limited and disputed, the story quickly gained traction. According to Russini, multiple outlets pushed narratives that strayed far from reality.She addressed this directly, stating, “In the days that followed, unfortunately, commentators in various media have engaged in self-feeding speculation that is simply unmoored from the facts,” Russini said in a letter sent Tuesday to Athletic Executive Editor Steven Ginsberg.Rather than allow the story to grow further, Russini chose to step away. Her decision appears rooted in a desire to prevent additional noise from overshadowing her work.This is a break in what has been a strong and steady career. Russini became known as a reliable NFL insider from her early days in New York to her rise at ESPN and later The Athletic. This chapter ends suddenly, but her voice in sports journalism isn't going away anytime soon.
Article Summary: Dianna Russini resigned from The Athletic following controversy linked to Mike Vrabel. In her resignation letter, she strongly defended her work, stating she stands by her reporting and professionalism. Russini criticized media speculation, calling it inaccurate and detached from facts.

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: Cowboys’ latest update complicates plans for Chargers, Patriots, and others
Article Text:
Trade speculation around the Dallas Cowboys has cooled after a surprising internal update revealed no teams have expressed interest in a potential deal. This shift leaves franchises like the Los Angeles Chargers, New England Patriots, San Francisco 49ers, and Baltimore Ravens reassessing their plans. With uncertainty growing, interested teams must decide whether to act quickly or wait. The situation highlights how timing and initiative can shape outcomes in a competitive NFL trade market.

Cowboys’ silence on George Pickens trade talks puts pressure on Chargers and other suitors

Trade buzz around the Dallas Cowboys has taken an unexpected turn, and it’s not the kind of update several receiver-needy teams were hoping for. While speculation had linked George Pickens to multiple contenders, the latest update suggests there may not be much movement at all. That leaves franchises like the Los Angeles Chargers and New England Patriots in a tricky spot as they reassess their offseason plans.Pickens, who has quickly grown into a high-impact target alongside Dak Prescott , is reportedly seeking long-term security. Yet, instead of intensifying trade talks, the situation has cooled. For teams that were quietly monitoring his availability, this development shifts the narrative from opportunity to uncertainty almost overnight.Despite outside belief that Pickens could be moved, the Cowboys appear in no rush to entertain offers. The most telling update came straight from inside the organization. “(Cowboys executive) Stephen Jones said on 1053thefan that so far the Cowboys have ‘had no one call with interest in George Pickens,’ regarding a potential trade,” ESPN’s Todd Archer reported on X/Twitter Tuesday.That statement changes the tone completely. Teams like the San Francisco 49ers and Baltimore Ravens, both of whom could benefit from another vertical threat, now face a waiting game rather than a bidding war.The Chargers feel like they need to act even faster. The team has shown some promise at wide receiver, especially with young players like Ladd McConkey. There are still questions about how deep and stable it will be in the long run. Adding a proven playmaker like Pickens, who had a great season with over 1,400 yards and nine touchdowns, could make the whole offense better.But here’s the catch. If no one is making calls, the window may not even be open yet.That puts the pressure back on interested teams. Do they wait and risk missing out, or make the first move and test Dallas’ resolve? In a league where timing often decides everything, hesitation could cost more than just a player.
Article Summary: George Pickens has been linked to a number of teams, but no one has expressed interest in a potential deal. The Cowboys appear in no rush to entertain offers for the wide receiver. That puts the pressure back on interested teams to act quickly or wait.
